AP Fertilizer Master | Weekly consumption planner

Kharif FY 26-27 vs FY 25-26 · IFMS sales, mandal-wise week-wise · Season allotment anchored to last year · Data to —

What needs attention —

Generated from the numbers below; recalculates when you switch product.

    State weekly trend —

    Bars: this year's actual weekly sales. Solid line: last year, same weeks. Dashed: what last year did in the weeks now ahead — the shape of demand to expect.

    FY 26-27 actual FY 25-26 same week FY 25-26 — weeks ahead

    Districts at a glance —

    Season to date vs last year same period, allotment used, and the load coming in the next 4 weeks. Tap a district to drill down.

    Sort by
    How to read: "Pace" compares this year's season-to-date with last year's same period — red = running ahead of last year (pressure on a fixed allotment), blue = behind. "Next 4 wks" is what the district sold in the identical 4 weeks last Kharif.

    Ease-out glide path —

    With allotment fixed to last year, a district that has front-loaded cannot repeat last year's remaining pattern. Dashed red line: what the district did in these weeks last Kharif. Blue bars: the weekly ceiling that lands exactly on the allotment.

    District

    District allotment position —

    Allotment = the district's full Kharif 25-26 consumption. Coverage = balance left ÷ what last year's pattern still demands ahead. Below 100%, offtake must ease; the last column is the ceiling as a share of last year's weekly pattern. Sorted worst first.

    Reading the flags: overshoot risk coverage under 90% — cannot follow last year's pattern; enforce the ease-out ceiling and tighten POS-level control now. tight coverage 90–105% — no slack; watch weekly. headroom coverage above 105% — can absorb a surge, and a potential source if re-appropriation is ever considered.

    Mandal watchlist — burning allotment fastest

    Mandals that have already consumed the largest share of their own last-Kharif total (small mandals filtered out). These are where saturation-driven or speculative buying shows up first — verify against POS data and crop coverage.

    Coming weeks — expected offtake by district ()

    Week-by-week demand ahead, anchored on last Kharif's actuals for the identical weeks. Darker cells = heavier expected offtake (shading relative to each district's own season). Keep stocks positioned before the dark cells.

    Horizon
    Basis

    Mandals carrying the next 4 weeks

    Largest expected mandal-level offtake in the coming 4 weeks (last-year basis) — where stock must physically be.

    Peak week watch

    Districts whose single heaviest week of last Kharif falls within the next 4 weeks — surge preparation needed now.

    Source: IFMS (GoI) fertilizer sales, mandal-wise week-wise · FY 25-26 full Kharif (Apr–Sep) and FY 26-27 Apr 1 – Jul 12 · Quantities in MT. Season and district allotments are anchored to FY 25-26 actual consumption, per current allocation policy. K Gangavaram (Konaseema) is included in all tables and district totals but has no boundary on the map. Vijayawada urban mandals share one map shape. Prepared for the Commissionerate of Agriculture, Govt. of Andhra Pradesh.
